full_prompt: Design a topology-optimized lower control arm for an open-source passenger-vehicle lightweighting benchmark. The arm replaces a baseline forged steel lower control arm of 4.5 kg on a mid-size B-segment passenger car with a 1450 kg curb mass at the front axle, redesigned in aluminum alloy 7075-T6 (yield 503 MPa, ultimate 572 MPa, E 71.7 GPa, density 2810 kg/m^3). The arm has three mounting interfaces: a ball joint to the steering knuckle (25 mm ID), and two bushings to the subframe (40 mm ID each, spaced 300 mm apart), with an overall envelope of 420 mm x 220 mm x 120 mm. The arm must simultaneously survive four independent industry-standard durability load cases: (LC1) longitudinal pothole 3x vertical wheel load = 9.0 kN at the ball joint horizontal, (LC2) lateral cornering 2.5x vertical wheel load = 7.5 kN at the ball joint laterally, (LC3) vertical bump 3.5x static wheel load = 10.5 kN vertical at the ball joint, (LC4) braking 2.0x wheel load = 6.0 kN longitudinal plus vertical. Pass/fail criteria: (R1) max von Mises stress under any of LC1-LC4 must not exceed 335 MPa (7075-T6 yield 503 / SF 1.5), (R2) max ball-joint displacement under any load case must not exceed 2.0 mm, (R3) arm mass must be at least 35% lighter than the 4.5 kg baseline forged steel arm, so <= 2.93 kg, (R4) a linear buckling analysis under LC4 braking must yield a first-mode load factor of at least 2.0, and (R5) a modal analysis must show the first bending mode above 200 Hz to avoid chassis-frequency coupling. Vibration (modal) analysis is required for R5. Thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are not required. Deliver a STEP AP242 solid body plus a report with max von Mises per load case, ball-joint displacement per load case, arm mass, first buckling load factor, first natural frequency, and mass-reduction percent vs baseline.
short_prompt:
Topology-optimize a front-axle lower control arm for a mid-size B-segment passenger car (1450 kg curb mass) in 7075-T6 aluminum, replacing a 4.5 kg forged-steel baseline. Design envelope is 420 x 220 x 120 mm; the arm has three mounting interfaces: a 25 mm ball joint to the steering knuckle and two 40 mm subframe bushings spaced 300 mm apart. Bushings are fixed cylindrical constraints; the ball joint is the load-application point.

Load cases (wheel-load factors).
- LC1 pothole longitudinal: 9.0 kN at the ball joint.
- LC2 lateral cornering: 7.5 kN at the ball joint.
- LC3 vertical bump: 10.5 kN at the ball joint.
- LC4 braking: 6.0 kN combined longitudinal + vertical.
- LC5 modal: first bending mode must stay above the chassis-frequency band.

Arm mass target is at least 35% lighter than the 4.5 kg baseline. Buckling margin is checked under LC4.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: 35% mass reduction target on a monolithic arm is the domain of topology optimization calling structural FEA inside.

prompt.title: Aluminum 7075-T6 lower control arm (lightweighting benchmark)
prompt.brief: Topology-optimize a 7075-T6 aluminum lower control arm for a 1450 kg B-segment car with 25 mm ball joint + 2x40 mm bushings, replacing a 4.5 kg forged steel baseline, passing 4 load cases, modal, and buckling with 35% mass reduction.

objective.primary: minimize mass while passing all load cases
objective.secondary: at least 35% reduction vs 4.5 kg steel baseline

out_of_scope_but_real_world_needed: Multi-axial fatigue (SAE AR8X, RLDA road load durability)
